Applications include high-performance computing for AI, smart phones, wearables,… Read More <

items.title: 【動物農莊】鴿們聊三星獎金制度　反而逼走晶圓代工人才？
items.summary: 三星電子（Samsung Electronics）晶圓代工事業部恐面臨人才流失危機。根據韓媒每日經濟報導，三星最大工會「超企業工會」針對半導體暨裝置解決方案（DS）部門逾8,000名員工進行調查，其中晶圓代工事業部高達81.5%

items.title: 雷射光源外置推波CPO大浪　中系供應鏈出招「繞道」延緩落地
items.summary: AI資料中心持續推升高速傳輸需求，儘管共同封裝光學（CPO）放量時程未能快速推動，但業界認為，電訊號傳輸終將逼近物理極限，CPO架構優勢仍是重要產業發展趨勢，且可藉由將雷射光源放置在封裝外部，不僅便於更換，也能供應至多個傳輸通道。值得注意的是，受限半導體技術支援的中系供應鏈，積極發展液冷設計以延緩CPO落地，試圖在地

items.title: 隱形冠軍40年砌成「最複雜機器」EUV護城河　一張照片見證半導體產業新時代
items.summary: AI浪潮持續推升全球半導體投資，也讓極紫外光（EUV）微影技術的重要性再度攀升。作為ASML EUV曝光機CO₂雷射光源關鍵供應商，同時也是「隱形冠軍」的德國雷射大廠創浦（Trumpf），技術長Berthold

items.title: 評析：黃仁勳著眼日系巨頭工業數據　Cosmos主導實體AI世界入口
items.summary: NVIDIA執行長黃仁勳7月中旬旋風訪問日本，參與遊戲業巨頭SEGA的聯合發布活動、行報恩之旅，更力推下一代人工智慧（AI）核心戰略實體AI（PhysicalAI）技術合作，吸引日本產業界更掀起「實體AI元年」新熱潮。一句話概括，黃仁勳要集結所有在日本有頭有臉的工業巨頭，合力打造出一個NVIDIA實體AI資料中心，並將其

items.title: AI資料中心成大型動態負載　考驗供電系統穩定
items.summary: 隨著生成式AI快速發展，AI資料中心正成為全球電力需求成長最快速的來源之一，然與傳統資料中心不同，業界指出，AI運算龐大且具有高度波動特性，對於電網負載來說會是新挑戰，同時對大型用電設備本身來說也必須具備更可靠的系統穩定能力。全球資料中心用電量預計至2030年將大幅增加約500TWh，其中近半數增量來自AI資料中心。龐大

items.title: 三星新設RX機器人事業部　由DX部門負責人盧泰文領軍
items.summary: 為將機器人培育成未來事業成長動能，三星電子（Samsung Electronics）新設機器人體驗（Robotics eXperience；RX）事業推動室，後續動向引起外界關注。根據韓媒ZDNet Korea、Theelec等報導，三星新成立

items.title: 燧原秀中國首顆CoPoS AI晶片樣品　布局先進封裝新技術
items.summary: 中國AI晶片業者燧原科技於2026世界人工智慧大會（WAIC 2026）期間宣布，與上海先封科技共同展示採用玻璃基CoPoS（Chip-on-Panel-on-

items.title: 台灣能源韌性再受考驗　綠電量價壓力傳導半導體供應鏈
items.summary: 台灣能源供應高度連動全球半導體產業發展，面對蘋果（Apple）、Google終端客戶要求使用淨零電力，台灣半導體供應鏈面臨層層減碳壓力。有業者採取綠電採購或統購策略，不過卻面臨「太陽能遇瓶頸、風電太慢」等困境。英特爾（Intel）前執行長Pat

items.title: CPO商轉揭矽光子封測新局　台OSAT各據山頭卡位2027放量
items.summary: 全球AI伺服器算力需求大爆發，資料中心規格加速升級的浪潮下，傳統以銅線傳輸訊號的路徑已逼近物理極限，隨著共同封裝光學（CPO）與相關解方邁入商轉元年，也帶動矽光子（SiPh）產業迎來爆發式成長。業界分析，矽光子晶片、光電零組件的獨立或整合封裝及測試，因涉及光電耦合、封裝散熱、光纖對位等挑戰，技術門檻不同於傳統流程，形成市

items.title: 壁仞1024卡NPO光互連超節點　轉向系統架構整合

items.title: 南韓ESG義務公告上線在即　不再「揭露愈多愈好」SDC、LGD怎應對？
items.summary: 南韓日前正式確定自2028年起將氣候資訊納入《資本市場法》法定公告範疇，且較2026年2月釋出的草案提前並擴大適用範圍。除了三星顯示器（Samsung Display, SDC）與樂金顯示器（LG Display, LGD）兩大廠須遵循法規，更牽動整體供應鏈的碳數據揭露壓力。自2028年起，連結資產10兆韓元（約67.

items.title: 三星Galaxy新機傳續押高通　Exynos翻身大計備受考驗
items.summary: 記憶體價格走揚，雖加重智慧型手機成本壓力，但市場傳出三星電子（Samsung Electronics）在新款Galaxy手機上，仍將大量採用高通（Qualcomm）晶片，而非先前市場預期的擴大採用自家行動應用處理器（AP）

items.title: 科技1分鐘：連續波雷射（Continuous Wave；CW Laser）
items.summary: 連續波雷射（Continuous Wave；CW Laser）為矽光子（Silicon Photonics）與共同封裝光學（CPO）技術的關鍵元件。從光收發模組、光引擎到AI資料中心，愈來愈多供應鏈開始圍繞CW Laser布局，因為AI光通訊的高速之下，每一道光訊號需要先具備穩定光源。CW

items.title: 評析：中東資金也搶進東協AI基建　堅守供應鏈「中立角色」方能勝出
items.summary: 從新加坡的角度觀察，整體東協AI發展具有「三不管地帶」與「國際樞紐」的雙元性質。熟悉AI資料中心供應鏈業者坦言，除了東協本地需求，中國市場也將成為東協AI產業鏈發展的另一項重要動能。在中美貿易戰、地緣政治的敏感局勢下，相關的系統整合廠、零組件製造體系，如果能夠守穩「AI基礎建設供應鏈」的中立角色，或許才能通吃兩大陣營的AI

items.title: AI算力吃電、發熱難題未解　工研院集結供應鏈找節能解方
items.summary: 工研院21日舉辦「半導體與AI算力永續競爭力論壇」。經濟部次長江文若開場致詞表示，AI算力對電力的需求快速成長下，如何讓半導體製造朝向更低碳、AI資料中心更節能的方向邁進，讓每度電的消耗都發揮發大效益是政府、廠商、法人要共同追求的目標。經濟部希望產業界投入低碳製造、以更佳的散熱設計落實在製程之中。工研院副院長胡竹生表示，AI

items.title: 曦智CPO、NPO光運算方案　加速多路布局AI光互連
items.summary: 中國AI晶片業者曦智科技（Lightelligence）於2026世界人工智慧大會（WAIC 2026）展示涵蓋CPO（Co-Packaged Optics，共封裝光學）、NPO（Near-Packaged

items.title: AI資料中心催生電網升級　日本啟動15GW電網擴建計畫
items.summary: 日本於2010年代推動再生能源發展時，便已意識到電網老舊，亟需更新與擴建。進入2020年代中期後，生成式AI與AI資料中心成為科技產業發展重心，日本能源政策也因此由過去偏向收縮轉為擴張。在AI伺服器、AI半導體及AI資料中心投資熱潮帶動下，日本各大電力公司正積極投入變電所與輸電網建設，以滿足AI時代快速成長的用電需求。日經

items.title: 三星、SK海力士、美光喊卡CXL控制器自研　憂自家產品互打
items.summary: 全球三大記憶體廠傳停止投入開發新一代半導體CXL（Compute Express Link）控制器，並轉向委外設計路線。分析認為，記憶體大廠擔憂若強行將CXL產品推向市場，可能蠶食通用型DRAM需求。根據韓媒ZDNet Korea引述業界消息，三星電子（Samsung Electronics）、SK海力士（SK

items.title: 三菱、東芝與羅姆拚9月整軍功率半導體　盼政府補助挑戰英飛凌
items.summary: 三菱電機（Mitsubishi Electric）正尋求在2026年9月，與功率半導體市場的競爭對手東芝（Toshiba）及羅姆半導體（Rohm）達成協議，整合三家公司旗下的功率半導體業務。彭博（Bloomberg）報導，隨著全球積極建置AI基礎設施，電子供應鏈中這一快速成長的市場需求大幅升溫。尤其隨著NVIDIA下一代Vera

items.title: The Architecture of Success: Why a Unified Semiconductor IP Strategy Is the Foundation of Modern SoC Design
items.summary: As architects push toward advanced nodes and increasingly demanding workloads, modern silicon design has entered an era of unprecedented complexity. A single design respin can cost millions of dollars and delay market entry. Against this backdrop, designs must be balanced for performance, bandwidth, power efficiency, security…

items.summary:
AI-powered platform identified four critical SoC bugs and helped prevent up to two weeks of potential development delays

Semiconductor verification is increasingly constrained not by simulation capacity, but by the engineering effort required to understand why a simulation failed. Modern ASIC and system-on-chip designs…

items.title: 傳台積電 2027 年調漲晶圓代工價格！先進、成熟製程最高漲 10%
items.summary: 市場消息傳出，台積電計畫自 2027 年起調漲晶圓代工價格，先進製程與成熟製程價格最高將調升 10%，目前台積 […]

items.title: Haesung DS lands CXMT as DDR5 substrate customer, weighs panel production for DDR6
items.summary: Haesung DS has reportedly added China's CXMT as a customer for DDR5 package substrates as the South Korean supplier expands its DDR5 business and considers panel-based production for higher-layer-count products expected with the DDR6 generation.

items.title: Goldkey secures NT$3.6 billion loan to expand AI server memory business
items.summary: Goldkey Technology has completed and signed a three-year syndicated loan worth NT$3.6 billion (US$111.3 million) to support inventory growth and working capital needs tied to AI servers and other high-demand markets. The Taiwan memory module maker said the financing will also back materials purchases as it prepares to expand its high-end RDIMM business in the second half of 2026.

items.title: Huawei Kirin 9030 shows SMIC's 7nm gains and ongoing efficiency gap
items.summary: A teardown of Huawei's Kirin 9030 flagship processor — built on SMIC's 7nm-class N+3 process — reveals continued density progress from China's leading chipmaker, but a significant efficiency gap against global peers remains.

items.title: Ishihara Sangyo doubles high-purity titanium dioxide capacity on AI server demand
items.summary: Ishihara Sangyo Kaisha plans to double production capacity for high-purity titanium dioxide used to make dielectric materials for multilayer ceramic capacitors, as rising AI server power requirements increase demand for the components.

items.title: Kimi K3 surge traces to Moonshot AI's GTC roadmap
items.summary: Moonshot AI's open-source large language model (LLM) Kimi K3 has surged in popularity after its launch, drawing attention across multiple model benchmarks and forcing the company to suspend new member subscriptions after a rapid influx of users strained computing capacity. A review of CEO Zhilin Yang's March 2026 technical talk at Nvidia GTC in San Jose, California, shows K3 was not an overnight hit, but the result of months of work on model architecture and training techniques.

items.title: 三星攜巴克萊首推聯名信用卡，在美搶攻金融服務市場
items.summary: 三星電子今天和英國巴克萊銀行合作推出在美國的首張聯名信用卡，深化在美國的金融服務領域布局，搶攻數位錢包，同時也 […]

items.title: 小摩 CEO：市場低估風險、現階段非投資良機
items.summary: 近期市場震盪劇烈，摩根大通（JPMorgan Chase，通稱小摩）執行長戴蒙（Jamie Dimon）認為， […]

items.title: 輝達揭 9% 持股  Nebius 盤後嗨、新興雲端股同歡
items.summary: 荷蘭 AI 基礎建設供應商 Nebius Group 在最新公布的監管文件顯示，輝達（Nvidia Corp. […]

items.title: Samsung, SK Hynix and Micron pull back on in-house CXL controller plans
items.summary: Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix and Micron Technology are reportedly scaling back or shelving plans to commercialize internally developed Compute Express Link (CXL) controller chips, shifting more controller work to specialist fabless suppliers while continuing to develop CXL-based memory products.

items.title: 大摩喊記憶體遭錯殺  SK 海力士、三星、美光回神
items.summary: 美光（Micron Technology）等美國記憶體、儲存概念股迎來反彈，分析人士認為，當前產業循環，可能存 […]

items.title: 甲骨文資料中心電力部署延宕？Bloom Energy 重挫
items.summary: 市場傳出，甲骨文（Oracle）的天然氣管線申請遭到墨西哥州政府駁回，可能衝擊資料中心電力建設、並影響合作夥伴 […]

items.title: Middlemen emerge as ASEAN's No. 2 AI compute buyers
items.summary: In the AI computing race, Nvidia AI server chips remain red-hot, even as the US-China trade confrontation shows no sign of easing. Although Shopee and YTL Group are among the biggest buyers of AI chips for data centers, industry sources familiar with ASEAN semiconductor distribution say many mid-sized "middlemen" have effectively become ASEAN's second-largest compute buyers, lending fresh credibility to earlier claims of black-market GPU smuggling and proxy buyers.

items.title: China's EUV gap keeps semiconductor catch-up in check
items.summary: As the US continues to tighten export controls on advanced semiconductor equipment to China, questions are mounting over whether China can narrow the gap with leading-edge manufacturing through mature nodes and advanced packaging. German manufacturer Zeiss said advanced packaging can improve overall chip performance, but it cannot fully replace the process advantages delivered by extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography.

items.title: AI ASIC、矽光子開始放量，矽格迎新廠投產紅利
items.summary: ASIC、矽光子、高效能運算（HPC）及記憶體測試需求持續升溫。法人指出，IC 封測廠矽格受惠新廠投產、產品開 […]

items.title: China's Z.ai reportedly powers up 1GW data center built entirely on domestic chips
items.summary: Chinese AI developer Z.ai has completed a giant data center running exclusively on domestically made chips, a milestone in Beijing's drive to replace restricted Nvidia silicon for future AI development.

items.title: DDR5 module spot prices hit record highs as China-made dies narrow price gap
items.summary: The pace of memory contract price increases has begun to slow in the second half of 2026, but DDR5 spot prices continue to climb as AI and server applications absorb available supply, according to industry sources.

items.title: 總頻寬上看 7.6Pbps！傳輝達祕密收購全美「暗光纖」，背後有何盤算？
items.summary: 隨著 AI 資料中心規模持續擴大，除了 GPU 外，網路基礎建設也成為競爭關鍵。市場消息傳出，輝達正悄悄在美國 […]

items.title: Taiwan launches AI- and IoT-powered smart heat protection network, starting with Tainan pilot
items.summary: As climate change fuels increasingly severe heat waves, Taiwan is stepping up efforts to combat heat-related illness by integrating artificial intelligence (AI), IoT sensors and social welfare services into a smart heat protection network to better protect vulnerable residents.

items.title: WAIC 2026 elevates the 'token factory' — why is China chasing cheap tokens over bigger models?
items.summary: At the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) in Shanghai, held July 17-20 across "three districts and four venues" with more than 1,100 exhibitors, one phrase recurred across vendor booths and keynotes: the "token factory".

items.title: TSMC weighs margin hit against long-term AI payoff in US push
items.summary: TSMC has announced an additional US$100 billion investment in the US on top of its existing US$165 billion commitment, expanding its Arizona footprint to 12 fabs and one R&D center. While the market has focused on the size of the spending, investors are more concerned that overseas expansion could squeeze profitability.

items.title: Li Auto reportedly forms new Shanghai chip company to expand in-house semiconductor ambitions
items.summary: Chinese electric vehicle (EV) maker Li Auto has established a new subsidiary focused on IC chip design, underscoring its long-term strategy of developing core AI technologies in-house.

items.title: Taiwan indicts ex-TSMC employees in first China-linked national core tech espionage case
items.summary: Taiwan prosecutors have indicted four former TSMC employees for allegedly stealing confidential semiconductor technology with the intent of transferring it to China, marking the island's first criminal prosecution involving "national core technologies" under the National Security Act. According to Nikkei Asia, the case highlights Taiwan's intensifying efforts to safeguard its semiconductor leadership amid growing competition from China.

items.title: Samsung consolidates robot teams and weighs standalone HX division
items.summary: Samsung Electronics has started a broad reorganization of its robot operations, bringing scattered teams under one structure as humanoid robot competition shifts toward artificial intelligence-based autonomous control. The move was reported by JoongAng Daily, which cited industry sources and said Samsung is aiming to centralize talent and technology across its robotics work.

items.title: China's AI supernode race seeks systems edge through optics, packaging, and cloud scale
items.summary: China's AI hardware race is shifting from individual accelerators to supernodes, optical interconnects, advanced packaging and cloud-scale systems.

items.title: BOE expands into glass-core packaging, solar cells and optical interconnects
items.summary: BOE Technology Group said on July 20 that it is pushing beyond display panels into glass-core substrate packaging, perovskite solar cells and Micro LED optical interconnects. The Chinese display maker outlined the three areas as strategic priorities as it moves toward advanced packaging, optoelectronics and new energy markets, according to meeting notes from an investor relations event posted on the Shanghai Stock Exchange website.

items.title: Niche demand drives SLC NAND prices up nearly tenfold, opening further gains for Taiwan suppliers
items.summary: Memory price increases have begun to diverge in the second half of 2026, but persistent capacity shortfalls continue to constrain supply and sustain upward momentum in contract prices.

items.title: How AI boom spurs Taiwan's PCB makers to rethink their Southeast Asia playbook
items.summary: Amid the global boom in AI infrastructure investment, Southeast Asia is evolving from an electronics-component manufacturing base into a critical node for AI servers, high-speed networking equipment and semiconductor supply chains. The transition is also serving as a major catalyst for the region's PCB industry to move up the value chain.

items.title: Pat Gelsinger: Intel missed GPU boom, warns Taiwan energy risk
items.summary: Former Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has criticized Intel's past strategy in a recent interview, revisiting the company's history with Nvidia and Apple while also weighing in on Taiwan Strait tensions and the outlook for quantum computing. Speaking at the RAISE Summit AI conference in Paris, he said Intel dismissed Nvidia GPUs at the height of its CPU dominance as nothing more than graphics tools for gamers.

items.title: Memory shortage could turn into a glut by 2028 as chipmakers expand
items.summary: A powerful rally in memory-chip stocks has stalled as investors reconsider whether record spending on AI data centers could eventually push the DRAM market back into oversupply.

items.title: Microsoft expands Azure partnership with AMD ahead of Helios AI system launch
items.summary: Microsoft has expanded its strategic partnership with AMD, announcing plans to deploy the company's upcoming Helios rack-scale AI system across Azure, a move that comes just days before AMD is expected to unveil its next-generation AI products at its Advancing AI 2026 event, which begins on July 22.

items.title: TaiSol expects server revenue to top 50% in 2H26, liquid cooling to exceed 25% for full year
items.summary: As power consumption of artificial intelligence (AI) servers continues to rise, liquid cooling is rapidly evolving from an auxiliary solution into a standard feature at the rack and data center levels. Optimistic that the liquid cooling trend will continue to grow, thermal solutions provider TaiSol Electronics said during its earnings call on July 20 that its liquid cooling portfolio has further expanded beyond cold plates, manifolds, and tubing to include coolant distribution units (CDU) and pr

items.title: Samsung keeps High-NA EUV rollout cautious as foundry profitability remains the focus
items.summary: Samsung Electronics is taking a slower approach than Intel to High-NA extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography for advanced chip production, weighing equipment spending against the need to restore foundry profitability. According to Chosun Biz, citing industry sources, the debate now centers less on yield than on financial returns.

items.title: Weblisher plans double-digit growth as Taiwan SME and Japan lodging businesses expand
items.summary: Weblisher, a data technology platform provider, is set to list on Taiwan's Emerging Stock Market on July 23. The company said 2026 revenue is expected to keep growing at a double-digit pace, driven by expansion in its Taiwan small and midsize business customer base and its Japan lodging management operations.

items.title: TSMC sees structural AI growth, expands Arizona capex
items.summary: TSMC Chief Financial Officer Wendell Huang said the company expects AI chip demand to remain strong for years, prompting it to keep expanding investment in its Arizona site. After previously announcing an additional US$100 billion investment, TSMC has raised its total planned investment there to US$265 billion.

items.title: Samsung, SK Hynix prioritize DDR5 as margins near HBM levels
items.summary: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are reportedly allocating additional or flexible DRAM production capacity to server DDR5 and other conventional memory products while maintaining high-bandwidth memory (HBM) volumes already committed to customers.

items.title: From Beijing to Singapore—how Beyondsoft is betting on agentic and physical AI in Southeast Asia
items.summary: Agentic AI is likely to reach commercial use sooner than physical AI, according to Beyondsoft, as the IT services provider deepens its push into robotics and enterprise automation. The Singapore-based company sees the region, especially Singapore, as a key proving ground where cloud partners, governments, and enterprises are shaping the next phase of AI adoption.

items.title: Glass substrates poised to be chip packaging's next battleground: Trumpf
items.summary: AI and high-performance computing are pushing chip packages beyond the limits of conventional materials, and German laser equipment maker Trumpf says that shift could accelerate demand for glass substrates and through-glass vias. The company said 12-inch wafers are nearing physical constraints, leaving advanced packaging in search of larger, more capable platforms.

items.title: Samsung could overtake SK Hynix in the HBM market by 2027
items.summary: Samsung Electronics could overtake SK Hynix to lead the global high-bandwidth memory market in 2027 as shipments of HBM4 expand, according to forecasts from UBS cited by Money Today and Financial News.

items.title: Zeiss accelerates push into AI chip advanced packaging, CPO
items.summary: Zeiss is moving into a new AI chip battleground as advanced packaging and silicon photonics rise on the back of chiplet-based architectures, multi-chip integration, and high-bandwidth memory (HBM). The company says it is extending decades of lithography expertise into advanced packaging, metrology, inspection, and co-packaged optics (CPO) to build competitiveness for the next decade.

items.title: AI-related power management needs drive e-Fuse demand for Fortune Advanced Technology
items.summary: Power management IC (PMIC) maker Fortune Advanced Technology is set to list on the Emerging Stock Board on July 22, with an eye on growing demand for high-voltage power management in AI data centers driving the need for e-fuses and smart power management solutions. In addition, shipments of higher-margin products are also lifting revenue for the company, as it seeks opportunities in importing substitute protective components.

items.title: AI boom may reshape SK Siltron sale as chairman reportedly rethinks
items.summary: SK Group chairman Chey Tae-Won is reportedly making a final decision on the sale of semiconductor silicon wafer subsidiary SK Siltron, as surging AI chip demand raises doubts about whether the asset should be sold at all. While SK originally pushed the sale to improve rebalancing and its financial structure, many inside and outside the group now see securing a stable wafer supply chain as the higher strategic priority.

items.title: VSMC warns AI-ready MES is becoming essential for wafer fabs, with China accelerating adoption
items.summary: TSMC's sustained capital spending highlights the two foundations of its competitive strength: technology leadership and manufacturing excellence. The latter remains a core advantage not only for TSMC but also for the broader semiconductor ecosystem built around its production model.

items.title: Keysight Design Engineering Software at DAC 2026: Going Deep on Chiplets, RF and AI-Driven Design
items.summary: Keysight Technologies returns to 2026 DAC, The Chips to Systems Conference this year as a Silver Sponsor and an “I LOVE DAC” sponsor, bringing its Design Engineering Software portfolio to Booth #927 at DAC 2026, which runs July 26-29 in Long Beach

items.title: HBM4 battle shifts to cooling as hybrid bonding slips
items.summary: Sixth-generation high-bandwidth memory (HBM4) shipments paired with Nvidia Vera Rubin will keep rising in the second half of 2026, setting up a head-to-head fight among the three major memory makers over stable mass production and supply. Industry sources say the next wave of high-capacity HBM will be decided by one core issue: how effectively vendors solve heat dissipation.
